mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 4.5% within a single monthly report, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds 4.5% in a monthly release
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial output and a sharp drop in consumer credit indicates a demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls >1.5% MoM and RSAFS falls >2% MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 60-day returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising core inflation erodes real yields and forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative when both inflation and policy rates are elevated, or the signal produces positive returns in >40% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real gross domestic product indicates expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for quarterly GDP growth vs 60-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in nonfarm payrolls above 250,000 per month signals an overheating labor market that historically precedes wage-push inflation and subsequent equity volatility, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower following months with payroll growth above 250k, or the signal's hit rate drops below 50% in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises while housing starts fall, it reflects a sectoral rotation from construction to manufacturing that typically coincides with peak corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises >0.5% MoM while HOUST falls >5% MoM
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to produce a positive predictive relationship for 20-day returns in >60% of observed instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion, rising consumer credit balances fuel discretionary spending that directly boosts equity earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises >1% MoM and CPIAUCSL remains below 3.0% YoY
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral 60-day forward returns in >50% of triggered windows, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
